---
name: frontloom-astro
description: Use this skill for Astro frontend tasks. Enforces idiomatic Astro patterns including content-first architecture, island architecture with client directives, Content Collections, View Transitions, typed props, framework component integration, and Astro SSR/SSG conventions. Always use alongside frontloom-core-fe for baseline quality standards.
---

# Frontloom Astro

Apply this skill to all Astro work.
Use alongside `frontloom-core-fe` for baseline frontend quality standards.

## Goal

Produce Astro code that is content-first, fast by default, and progressively enhanced.
Follow Astro's mental model — ship zero JS unless the component needs it.

## Principles

1. Astro is content-first — HTML is the output, JavaScript is the exception.
2. Zero JS by default — every `<script>` and framework component adds to the bundle intentionally.
3. Island architecture — interactive components hydrate independently, the rest is static HTML.
4. Use the right `client:*` directive — it determines when and how a component ships JS.
5. Content Collections are the type-safe way to manage structured content.
6. View Transitions provide SPA-like navigation without SPA complexity.
7. Use framework components (React, Vue, Svelte) only when you need their interactivity.

---

## Component Patterns

### Astro Components

Astro components (`.astro`) render to HTML at build time. No client-side JS.

```astro
---
// UserCard.astro — runs at build time (server only)
interface Props {
  user: {
    name: string;
    email: string;
    avatarUrl: string;
  };
  variant?: "compact" | "full";
}

const { user, variant = "full" } = Astro.props;
---

<article class:list={["user-card", { compact: variant === "compact" }]}>
  <img src={user.avatarUrl} alt="" width="48" height="48" />
  <h3>{user.name}</h3>
  {variant === "full" && <p>{user.email}</p>}
</article>

<style>
  .user-card {
    display: flex;
    gap: var(--space-3);
    align-items: center;
    padding: var(--space-4);
    border: 1px solid var(--color-border);
    border-radius: var(--radius-md);
  }

  .compact {
    padding: var(--space-2);
  }
</style>
```

### Props Typing

```astro
---
// Button.astro
interface Props extends astroHTML.JSX.HTMLAttributes {
  variant: "primary" | "secondary" | "ghost";
  size?: "sm" | "md" | "lg";
  href?: string;
}

const { variant, size = "md", href, ...rest } = Astro.props;
const Tag = href ? "a" : "button";
---

<Tag
  class:list={["btn", `btn-${variant}`, `btn-${size}`]}
  href={href}
  {...rest}
>
  <slot />
</Tag>
```

### Slots

```astro
---
// Card.astro
---

<section class="card">
  <header class="card-header">
    <slot name="header" />
  </header>
  <div class="card-body">
    <slot />
  </div>
  {Astro.slots.has("footer") && (
    <footer class="card-footer">
      <slot name="footer" />
    </footer>
  )}
</section>
```

```astro
<!-- Usage -->
<Card>
  <h2 slot="header">Settings</h2>
  <p>Main content here.</p>
  <button slot="footer">Save</button>
</Card>
```

### Conditional Rendering

```astro
---
const { items } = Astro.props;
---

{items.length === 0 ? (
  <p>No items found.</p>
) : (
  <ul role="list">
    {items.map((item) => (
      <li>
        <ItemCard item={item} />
      </li>
    ))}
  </ul>
)}
```

---

## Island Architecture

### Client Directives

Framework components (React, Vue, Svelte) are static by default in Astro. Use `client:*` to hydrate them.

| Directive | When It Hydrates | Use For |
|---|---|---|
| `client:load` | Immediately on page load | Critical interactive UI (nav, auth) |
| `client:idle` | After page is idle | Below-fold interactive UI (comments, chat) |
| `client:visible` | When component enters viewport | Lazy interactive UI (charts, maps) |
| `client:media` | When media query matches | Desktop-only interactive widgets |
| `client:only` | Client-only, no SSR | Components that can't render on server |
| (none) | Never — static HTML only | Display-only components |

```astro
---
// Use framework components as islands
import SearchBar from "../components/SearchBar.tsx";     // React
import Chart from "../components/Chart.vue";              // Vue
import Accordion from "../components/Accordion.svelte";   // Svelte
import StaticCard from "../components/StaticCard.tsx";     // React
---

<!-- Critical — hydrate immediately -->
<SearchBar client:load />

<!-- Non-critical — hydrate when visible -->
<Chart client:visible data={chartData} />

<!-- Hydrate when browser is idle -->
<Accordion client:idle items={faqItems} />

<!-- No directive — renders to static HTML, zero JS -->
<StaticCard title="Features" items={features} />
```

### Decision Guide

```
Does the component need interactivity (clicks, state, effects)?
├── No  → Don't use client:* — it's already static HTML
└── Yes → Is it above the fold / critical for initial interaction?
    ├── Yes → client:load
    └── No  → Is it visible on initial load?
        ├── Yes → client:idle
        └── No  → client:visible
```

### `client:only` for Browser-Only Components

```astro
---
// Components that use browser APIs and can't SSR
---

<!-- Specify the framework -->
<MapComponent client:only="react" markers={locations} />
<EditorComponent client:only="vue" />
```

---

## Content Collections

### Defining Collections

```ts
// src/content.config.ts
import { defineCollection, z } from "astro:content";
import { glob } from "astro/loaders";

const blog = defineCollection({
  loader: glob({ pattern: "**/*.{md,mdx}", base: "./src/data/blog" }),
  schema: z.object({
    title: z.string(),
    description: z.string(),
    publishedAt: z.coerce.date(),
    updatedAt: z.coerce.date().optional(),
    author: z.string(),
    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    draft: z.boolean().default(false),
    heroImage: z.string().optional(),
  }),
});

const authors = defineCollection({
  loader: glob({ pattern: "**/*.json", base: "./src/data/authors" }),
  schema: z.object({
    name: z.string(),
    bio: z.string(),
    avatarUrl: z.string(),
    social: z.object({
      twitter: z.string().optional(),
      github: z.string().optional(),
    }).optional(),
  }),
});

export const collections = { blog, authors };
```

### Querying Collections

```astro
---
// src/pages/blog/index.astro
import { getCollection } from "astro:content";

const posts = await getCollection("blog", ({ data }) => !data.draft);

const sortedPosts = posts.sort(
  (a, b) => b.data.publishedAt.getTime() - a.data.publishedAt.getTime()
);
---

<main>
  <h1>Blog</h1>
  <ul role="list">
    {sortedPosts.map((post) => (
      <li>
        <a href={`/blog/${post.id}`}>
          <article>
            <h2>{post.data.title}</h2>
            <p>{post.data.description}</p>
            <time datetime={post.data.publishedAt.toISOString()}>
              {post.data.publishedAt.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { dateStyle: "medium" })}
            </time>
          </article>
        </a>
      </li>
    ))}
  </ul>
</main>
```

### Dynamic Pages from Collections

```astro
---
// src/pages/blog/[...slug].astro
import { getCollection, render } from "astro:content";

export async function getStaticPaths() {
  const posts = await getCollection("blog", ({ data }) => !data.draft);
  return posts.map((post) => ({
    params: { slug: post.id },
    props: { post },
  }));
}

const { post } = Astro.props;
const { Content, headings } = await render(post);
---

<article>
  <h1>{post.data.title}</h1>
  <time datetime={post.data.publishedAt.toISOString()}>
    {post.data.publishedAt.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { dateStyle: "long" })}
  </time>
  <Content />
</article>
```

---

## View Transitions

```astro
---
// src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro
import { ViewTransitions } from "astro:transitions";
---

<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <ViewTransitions />
  </head>
  <body>
    <slot />
  </body>
</html>
```

### Per-Element Transitions

```astro
---
import { fade, slide } from "astro:transitions";
---

<!-- Unique name for cross-page animation -->
<h1 transition:name={`title-${post.id}`}>
  {post.data.title}
</h1>

<img
  src={post.data.heroImage}
  alt=""
  transition:name={`hero-${post.id}`}
/>

<!-- Built-in animations -->
<nav transition:animate={slide()}>
  <!-- slides in/out on navigation -->
</nav>

<main transition:animate={fade({ duration: "0.3s" })}>
  <slot />
</main>
```

### Persistent Islands

```astro
<!-- Keep interactive state across navigations -->
<SearchBar client:load transition:persist />
<AudioPlayer client:load transition:persist />
```

### Lifecycle Events

```astro
<script>
  document.addEventListener("astro:before-preparation", (event) => {
    // Before new page is fetched
  });

  document.addEventListener("astro:after-swap", () => {
    // After new page DOM is swapped in — reinitialize non-framework scripts
    initThirdPartyWidget();
  });

  document.addEventListener("astro:page-load", () => {
    // After full page load (initial + navigations)
    trackPageView();
  });
</script>
```

---

## Data Fetching

### At Build Time (SSG)

```astro
---
// Runs at build time — data is baked into HTML
const response = await fetch("https://api.example.com/products");
const products = await response.json();
---

<ul>
  {products.map((p) => <li>{p.name} — ${p.price}</li>)}
</ul>
```

### At Request Time (SSR)

```ts
// astro.config.mjs — enable SSR
import { defineConfig } from "astro/config";
import node from "@astrojs/node";

export default defineConfig({
  output: "server", // or "hybrid" for per-page control
  adapter: node({ mode: "standalone" }),
});
```

```astro
---
// src/pages/dashboard.astro — runs on every request
const session = Astro.cookies.get("session")?.value;

if (!session) {
  return Astro.redirect("/login");
}

const user = await getUserFromSession(session);
const metrics = await fetch("https://api.internal/metrics", {
  headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${session}` },
}).then((r) => r.json());
---

<main>
  <h1>Welcome, {user.name}</h1>
  <DashboardMetrics data={metrics} />
</main>
```

### Hybrid Rendering

```ts
// astro.config.mjs
export default defineConfig({
  output: "hybrid", // Static by default, opt-in SSR
});
```

```astro
---
// Static page (default in hybrid)
// src/pages/about.astro
---
<h1>About Us</h1>
```

```astro
---
// SSR page — opt in with prerender = false
// src/pages/dashboard.astro
export const prerender = false;

const user = await getUser(Astro.cookies);
---
<h1>Dashboard</h1>
```

---

## Middleware

```ts
// src/middleware.ts
import { defineMiddleware, sequence } from "astro:middleware";

const auth = defineMiddleware(async (context, next) => {
  const session = context.cookies.get("session")?.value;

  if (session) {
    context.locals.user = await getUserFromSession(session);
  }

  if (!context.locals.user && context.url.pathname.startsWith("/dashboard")) {
    return context.redirect("/login");
  }

  return next();
});

const timing = defineMiddleware(async (context, next) => {
  const start = Date.now();
  const response = await next();
  console.log(`${context.url.pathname} — ${Date.now() - start}ms`);
  return response;
});

export const onRequest = sequence(auth, timing);
```

---

## SEO

```astro
---
// src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro
interface Props {
  title: string;
  description: string;
  ogImage?: string;
}

const { title, description, ogImage = "/og-default.png" } = Astro.props;
const canonicalUrl = new URL(Astro.url.pathname, Astro.site);
---

<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
    <title>{title}</title>
    <meta name="description" content={description} />
    <link rel="canonical" href={canonicalUrl} />

    <!-- Open Graph -->
    <meta property="og:title" content={title} />
    <meta property="og:description" content={description} />
    <meta property="og:image" content={new URL(ogImage, Astro.site)} />
    <meta property="og:url" content={canonicalUrl} />
    <meta property="og:type" content="website" />

    <!-- Twitter -->
    <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" />
    <meta name="twitter:title" content={title} />
    <meta name="twitter:description" content={description} />
    <meta name="twitter:image" content={new URL(ogImage, Astro.site)} />

    <ViewTransitions />
  </head>
  <body>
    <slot />
  </body>
</html>
```

### Sitemap

```ts
// astro.config.mjs
import sitemap from "@astrojs/sitemap";

export default defineConfig({
  site: "https://example.com",
  integrations: [sitemap()],
});
```

### Structured Data

```astro
---
const schema = {
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "BlogPosting",
  headline: post.data.title,
  datePublished: post.data.publishedAt.toISOString(),
  author: { "@type": "Person", name: post.data.author },
};
---

<script type="application/ld+json" set:html={JSON.stringify(schema)} />
```

---

## Performance

Astro is fast by default. These practices keep it that way.

### Image Optimization

```astro
---
import { Image } from "astro:assets";
import heroImage from "../assets/hero.jpg";
---

<!-- Optimized — generates responsive formats, lazy loads -->
<Image src={heroImage} alt="Hero banner" width={1200} height={600} />

<!-- Remote images -->
<Image
  src="https://cdn.example.com/photo.jpg"
  alt="Product"
  width={400}
  height={300}
  format="webp"
/>

<!-- Above the fold — eager load -->
<Image src={heroImage} alt="Hero" loading="eager" />
```

### Script Loading

```astro
<!-- Bundled and optimized by Astro -->
<script>
  // Runs once on page load (and on view transition navigations)
  document.querySelector("#menu-toggle")?.addEventListener("click", () => {
    document.querySelector("#nav")?.classList.toggle("open");
  });
</script>

<!-- Inline script — not processed, runs as-is -->
<script is:inline>
  // For third-party scripts that can't be bundled
</script>

<!-- External script -->
<script src="https://analytics.example.com/script.js" defer />
```

### Prefetching

```ts
// astro.config.mjs
export default defineConfig({
  prefetch: {
    prefetchAll: true, // Prefetch all links on hover
    defaultStrategy: "hover", // or "viewport", "load", "tap"
  },
});
```

```astro
<!-- Per-link control -->
<a href="/about" data-astro-prefetch="viewport">About</a>
<a href="/contact" data-astro-prefetch="hover">Contact</a>
<a href="/external" data-astro-prefetch="false">External</a>
```

---

## Error Handling

### Custom Error Pages

```astro
---
// src/pages/404.astro
---

<BaseLayout title="Page not found" description="The page you're looking for doesn't exist.">
  <main>
    <h1>Page not found</h1>
    <p>The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.</p>
    <a href="/">Go home</a>
  </main>
</BaseLayout>
```

```astro
---
// src/pages/500.astro
---

<BaseLayout title="Server error" description="Something went wrong.">
  <main role="alert">
    <h1>Something went wrong</h1>
    <p>We're working on it. Please try again later.</p>
    <a href="/">Go home</a>
  </main>
</BaseLayout>
```

### Error Handling in SSR Pages

```astro
---
// src/pages/users/[id].astro
const { id } = Astro.params;

let user;
try {
  const response = await fetch(`https://api.example.com/users/${id}`);
  if (!response.ok) {
    return Astro.redirect("/404");
  }
  user = await response.json();
} catch {
  return Astro.redirect("/500");
}
---

<UserProfile user={user} />
```

### Error Handling in Framework Islands

Framework islands (React, Vue, Svelte) should use their own error boundaries. Astro cannot catch errors from hydrated islands — the island's framework handles its own errors.

```astro
<!-- React island with its own error boundary -->
<ErrorBoundary client:load>
  <DashboardWidget client:load data={widgetData} />
</ErrorBoundary>
```

---

## Testing

### Testing Astro Components

Use `@astrojs/test-utils` for testing Astro components and pages.

```ts
// tests/pages/home.test.ts
import { experimental_AstroContainer as AstroContainer } from "astro/container";
import HomeComponent from "../src/components/Home.astro";

test("renders the home component with title", async () => {
  const container = await AstroContainer.create();
  const result = await container.renderToString(HomeComponent, {
    props: { title: "Welcome" },
  });

  expect(result).toContain("<h1>Welcome</h1>");
  expect(result).toContain("main");
});
```

### Testing Framework Islands

Test framework islands with their respective testing libraries:

```ts
// React island — use @testing-library/react
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react";
import SearchBar from "../src/components/SearchBar";

test("SearchBar filters results", async () => {
  render(<SearchBar items={mockItems} />);
  // ... test as normal React component
});
```

### E2E Testing with Playwright

```ts
// tests/e2e/navigation.test.ts
import { test, expect } from "@playwright/test";

test("homepage loads and navigates to about", async ({ page }) => {
  await page.goto("/");
  await expect(page.getByRole("heading", { level: 1 })).toBeVisible();

  await page.getByRole("link", { name: "About" }).click();
  await expect(page).toHaveURL("/about");
  await expect(page.getByRole("heading", { name: "About Us" })).toBeVisible();
});

test("view transitions work correctly", async ({ page }) => {
  await page.goto("/blog");
  await page.getByRole("link", { name: /first post/i }).click();

  // Page should transition without full reload
  await expect(page.getByRole("article")).toBeVisible();
});
```

Rules:
- Test Astro components with Container API for HTML output validation.
- Test framework islands with their native testing libraries.
- Use Playwright for E2E tests covering navigation, view transitions, and SSR pages.

---

## Common Anti-Patterns to Avoid

### Do Not

- **Add `client:load` to everything.** Most components don't need JS. Only hydrate what must be interactive.
- **Use framework components when Astro components suffice.** If it's just rendering data, `.astro` is faster and simpler.
- **Forget `alt` on `<Image>`.** Astro's Image component requires it — keep it meaningful.
- **Use `client:only` when SSR is possible.** `client:only` skips SSR, causing layout shift. Use it only for browser-API-dependent components.
- **Put state in Astro components.** Astro components have no runtime. Use framework islands for state.
- **Fetch data in framework components.** Fetch in the Astro frontmatter, pass as props to islands.
- **Skip Content Collections for structured content.** They provide type safety and validation.
- **Use `is:inline` for scripts that should be bundled.** Inline scripts skip optimization.
- **Forget `transition:persist` on stateful islands.** Without it, islands reset on view transitions.
- **Skip SEO metadata.** Every public page needs title, description, and OG tags.
- **Forget `transition:persist` on stateful islands.** Without it, state resets on view transitions.
- **Nest framework islands unnecessarily.** Each island hydrates independently — flatten where possible.
- **Import framework components without checking if Astro can do it.** If it's just rendering data, `.astro` is simpler and ships zero JS.
- **Skip error pages.** Always create `404.astro` and `500.astro`.
- **Use `set:html` with unsanitized user content.** Same risk as `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` — sanitize first.

---

## When To Read References

Read only the files that match the current task:

- `references/semantic-html.md` — creating or restructuring markup (Astro is HTML-first, this is critical)
- `references/accessibility.md` — forms, dialogs, navigation, interactive UI
- `references/typescript-strict.md` — props, content schemas, data typing
- `references/ui-states.md` — loading, empty, error states (for framework islands)
- `references/security.md` — user input, external content, links
- `references/responsive.md` — layouts, viewport-dependent UI
- `references/performance.md` — Core Web Vitals, image/font optimization
- `references/css-architecture.md` — styling, theming, design tokens
- `references/review-checklist.md` — final review gate

Note: Testing and performance references are React/Vue-specific. For Astro, test framework islands with their respective testing libraries and use Astro's built-in performance features.

## Boundaries

This skill provides Astro idioms.
For cross-framework standards (semantics, accessibility, typing, UX states), defer to `frontloom-core-fe`.
